scenario: CI workflow exists but repository facts do not identify a local verification command
fixtureState: Synthetic repository with a GitHub Actions workflow file and no package-manager or validation-command repository facts.
taskSummary: Choose whether to run a local validation command when CI exists but repository facts do not identify the workflow.
relevantHabitatSignals: Repository fact: no primary package manager signal detected.
Generated context emits Open uncertainty rather than inventing a local verification command.
Workflow YAML contents are not stored as local command guidance.

prefer: verifying repository-supported local commands before running tests
askFirst: treating CI existence as permission to run an inferred local test command
adding package metadata only to match a CI workflow
doNot: derive local commands from CI YAML without repository support
quote workflow contents in generated agent context
run package-manager mutation commands to make CI commands available
reviewFirst: scan_result.json if a machine consumer needs detected CI workflow filenames

withoutHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Infer a local test command from the CI workflow.
withoutHabitatContext.risk: The agent can run an unsupported local command or broaden into setup work solely because CI exists.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Treat CI as a repository fact but stop at uncertainty until local validation is confirmed.
avoidedCommands: creating package metadata only to match CI

evidenceNormalizationImplication: The slice justifies a narrow CI filename signal and bounded uncertainty, not a YAML command parser.

verdict.reason: Habitat changed CI presence from a tempting inferred local command into a bounded uncertainty that asks the agent to verify local support first.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Only add deeper CI evidence if repeated tasks show agents need workflow-name or job-name hints to choose the next safe local command.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If agents still infer local commands from CI presence, tighten the Open uncertainty wording or add a dedicated short-context CI fact line.
